story.title: COVID-19 Oral History with Jani Revier
story.description: The interview, part of Idaho's COVID-19 oral history project, features Jani Revier, Director of the Idaho Department of Labor, detailing the department's challenges during the pandemic. Revier highlights the complexities of adapting to federal unemployment programs, like PUA and FPUC, amidst a surge in claims from 10,000 to 70,000, emphasizing the strain on systems not designed for rapid change. Strategies to combat fraud included identity verification processes. She reflects on navigating leadership under pressure, balancing integrity with urgent financial distribution, and adapting departmental processes for future resilience. Overall, the conversation underscores the unexpected burdens and learning experiences during a time of crisis.
